schema.appDescription: Calculate total property registration charges — stamp duty, registration fee, legal charges, and all other costs for buying property in India.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Property Registration Calculator.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: What documents are needed for property registration?
faq.a: Required: Sale deed (original), PAN card, Aadhaar card, passport photos (buyer + seller), property card/7-12 extract, society NOC, encumbrance certificate, tax receipts, and loan sanction letter if applicable. Some states need additional documents like Form 60/61 for purchases without PAN.

faq.q: How long does property registration take?
faq.a: Online registration (e-stamp + appointment): same day in most states. Physical registration at Sub-Registrar office: typically 1–3 days after document submission. Mutation (updating revenue records): 15–60 days after registration. Faster in states with e-registration (Maharashtra, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh).

faq.q: What is circle rate in property registration?
faq.a: Circle rate is the minimum value at which a property can be registered, set by the government. Even if actual transaction price is lower, stamp duty is calculated on circle rate. In major cities, circle rates are typically 10–40% below market value in prime areas.
No. Calculations run in your browser.

examples.heading: ₹50 lakh flat registration in Mumbai
examples.text: Stamp duty (6%) ₹3,00,000 + Registration ₹30,000 (capped) + Legal ₹15,000–₹25,000 = Total ₹3,45,000–₹3,55,000. Always budget 7–8% above property price for all charges including GST (if applicable) and society transfer fees.

examples.heading: Resale property vs new flat
examples.text: Resale flat has stamp duty on the full transaction value. New property (builder flat) has GST of 5% (or 1% for affordable housing). For a ₹1 crore new flat: GST ₹5 lakh + stamp duty ₹5 lakh + registration ₹50,000 = ₹10.5 lakh in additional charges.
